name: Steamed Pork Dumplings (Shiu Mai)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------SEASONING-------------------------
1/2 tsp Salt
1 tbsp Sugar
1 tbsp Sesame oil
2 ts Thin soy sauce
1 tbsp Oyster sauce
1 tbsp Cornstarch
Shell, devein, wash, and drain prawns. Dice into bits.
Boil mushrooms in water for 10 minutes, rinse, squeeze dry,
and chop off and discard stems; then chop into very small pieces.
Combine the pork, mushrooms, prawns, and onion. Put the mixture
on a chopping board and chop with a cleaver or sharp knife
until it is slightly finer than hamburger.
Add "seasoning" (salt, sugar, sesame oil, thin soy sauce,
oyster sauce, and cornstarch) to the pork mixture and mix well.
To make dumplings, place 1 tbsp filling in the center of a dumpling skin.
Bring all sides of the skin up to cover the meat as much as possible,
without closing. The top of the dumpling is left open.
Cook dumplings by steaming for 30 minutes. Use as many as you need;
the rest can be cooled, wrapped, and frozen. Reheat after thawing
by steaming for 10 minutes. Serve with soy sauce, hot sauce, or mustard.
Dumpling skins are similar to wonton skins; they are round and slightly thinner.
You may substitute wonton skins by cutting off the corners to round the skin.
